_meta.cycle_disposition_reason: Weekly research bundle contains three Tier 1 and Tier 2 developments: C3S El Niño confirmation with coral bleaching precursor, multi-source AMOC slowdown consolidation, and three-year 1.5C exceedance confirmation. Reasoner flags trajectory acceleration for AMOC and coral systems. Material change threshold met.

lead_signal.headline: C3S confirms developing moderate-to-strong El Niño with second-warmest March SST on record, opening compound bleaching and drought cascade risk window across Indo-Pacific and tropical Americas through boreal summer 2026
lead_signal.body: Copernicus Climate Change Service seasonal forecast confirms all multi-system components predict El Niño development with March 2026 recording second-warmest global sea surface temperature behind March 2025. Drier-than-average conditions forecast for southern and southeastern Asia, Central America, and northern South America overlap with major coral reef systems and Amazon drought stress zones. Trajectory implies compounding thermal stress window for coral systems already operating under structurally elevated baseline SSTs, consistent with fourth consecutive year of anomalous ocean heat.

planetary_status_snapshot.lead_signal: Three-year average 2023-2025 exceeds 1.5C for first time; sustained breach projected by February 2034, nine years ahead of 2015 baseline

planetary_boundary_tracker.week_development: C3S confirms three-year average 2023-2025 exceeded 1.5C for first time on record. March 2026 at 1.48C sustains near-threshold conditions. Projected sustained breach by February 2034, nine years earlier than 2015 Paris Agreement baseline projection. Structural acceleration signal, not single-year anomaly.
planetary_boundary_tracker.asymmetric: Nine-year acceleration of 1.5C breach timeline represents structural failure of NDC mitigation framework and creates compounding cascade risk across all other boundaries

planetary_boundary_tracker.key_indicator: Living Planet Index decline; tropical forest biome stress
planetary_boundary_tracker.week_development: El Niño drought forecast elevates stress on tropical forest biomes. No new Tier 1 biodiversity data this week. Trajectory continuing per baseline assessment.
planetary_boundary_tracker.asymmetric: El Niño-driven drought compounds existing biosphere stress in Amazon transition zones, creating non-linear risk of accelerated dieback
No binding international framework for nitrogen and phosphorus loading governance; attribution gap persists

planetary_boundary_tracker.key_indicator: Forest cover loss; Amazon transition zone stress
planetary_boundary_tracker.week_development: El Niño forecast implies elevated fire risk in Amazon transition zones. No new Tier 1 deforestation data this week. Trajectory continuing.
planetary_boundary_tracker.asymmetric: El Niño-driven fire risk in Amazon transition zones creates compounding pressure on already-transgressed land system boundary

planetary_boundary_tracker.key_indicator: Regional water stress; El Niño drought forecast
planetary_boundary_tracker.week_development: C3S El Niño forecast projects drier conditions in Asia and Central America, amplifying stress in chronic scarcity regions. No quantitative boundary data this week.
planetary_boundary_tracker.asymmetric: El Niño-driven drought in Central America Dry Corridor and Southeast Asia creates food-water-displacement cascade risk

planetary_boundary_tracker.week_development: El Niño SST anomalies and AMOC carbon feedback quantification imply continued pressure on aragonite states. No new direct measurement data this week.
planetary_boundary_tracker.asymmetric: AMOC collapse carbon feedback of 47-83 ppm CO2 would materially worsen ocean acidification trajectory through reduced carbon uptake capacity

planetary_boundary_tracker.key_indicator: Regional aerosol loading; IMO 2020 sulfur regulation impact
planetary_boundary_tracker.week_development: IMO 2020 sulfur cuts reduced aerosol cooling over Great Barrier Reef, adding 11 W per square meter radiation and increasing thermal stress by 5-10 percent. Regional decrease with ecological cost; no global boundary data for status change.
planetary_boundary_tracker.asymmetric: Ship sulfur regulation creates dual risk: reduced aerosol cooling increases coral bleaching risk while improving air quality. No international liability framework for cross-jurisdictional aerosol impacts.
Montreal Protocol success case; stable trajectory under existing governance framework

planetary_boundary_tracker.key_indicator: 140,000+ synthetic chemicals released; fewer than 3,000 tested for toxicity
planetary_boundary_tracker.week_development: Ship sulfur regulation reduces aerosols, increasing Great Barrier Reef thermal stress by 5-10 percent via novel pathway. Reinforces continuing trajectory; no status change.
planetary_boundary_tracker.asymmetric: No binding international framework for novel chemical governance. 140,000+ synthetic chemicals released with fewer than 3,000 tested for toxicity. Total attribution gap.

tipping_point_tracker.key_indicator: Western boundary decline across four mooring arrays; carbon feedback quantified at 47-83 ppm CO2
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_risk: Amazon dieback, Sahel drought intensification, South Asian monsoon disruption, coral reef collapse
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_chain: AMOC slowdown reduces tropical rainfall redistribution, intensifying drought in Sahel and monsoon disruption in South Asia, amplifying food insecurity and conflict risk. Carbon feedback of 47-83 ppm CO2 adds 0.2C warming, accelerating Amazon dieback and coral bleaching.
tipping_point_tracker.source: Science Advances 2026; Communications Earth and Environment 2026; Carbon Brief 2026
tipping_point_tracker.amoc_fovs_proxy: No FovS freshwater flux data retrieved this week
tipping_point_tracker.asymmetric: Multi-mooring array evidence consolidates AMOC slowdown signal. Carbon feedback pathway previously underexplored; quantification at 47-83 ppm CO2 materially alters long-run physical risk pricing and stranded asset timelines.

tipping_point_tracker.key_indicator: El Niño drought forecast for northern South America; 17-20 percent of Amazon already crossed tipping threshold per INPE 2024 baseline
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_risk: Climate change boundary acceleration, biosphere integrity collapse, land system change acceleration
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_chain: El Niño-driven drought increases fire risk in Amazon transition zones, accelerating deforestation and carbon release. Dieback reduces carbon sink capacity, increasing atmospheric CO2 and accelerating climate change boundary transgression.
tipping_point_tracker.source: Copernicus Climate Change Service 2026; INPE 2024 baseline
tipping_point_tracker.asymmetric: El Niño drought forecast creates compounding pressure on Amazon transition zones already operating under 17-20 percent partial tipping baseline. No new fire or deforestation data this week, but precursor signal strengthens.

tipping_point_tracker.cascade_risk: Sea level rise, AMOC freshwater flux disruption
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_chain: Greenland melt increases freshwater flux into North Atlantic, disrupting AMOC salinity gradient and accelerating AMOC slowdown. Sea level rise creates coastal infrastructure failure and displacement pressure.
tipping_point_tracker.asymmetric: No new mass balance or calving data this week. Background trajectory continues without new signals.

tipping_point_tracker.key_indicator: Ice shelf stability; grounding line retreat
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_risk: Sea level rise, Southern Ocean circulation disruption
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_chain: West Antarctic ice sheet collapse contributes multi-meter sea level rise over centuries, creating catastrophic coastal infrastructure failure and mass displacement. Southern Ocean circulation disruption alters global heat distribution.
tipping_point_tracker.asymmetric: No new ice shelf stability or grounding line data this week. Background trajectory continues without new signals.

tipping_point_tracker.key_indicator: Methane flux; thaw depth; GERP 15-20 percent upward revision active
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_chain: Permafrost thaw releases methane, a greenhouse gas 28 times more potent than CO2 over 100 years. Methane release accelerates climate change boundary transgression, creating positive feedback loop.
tipping_point_tracker.source: IPCC AR6 baseline; GERP 2024 upward revision
tipping_point_tracker.asymmetric: No new methane flux or thaw depth data this week. Sustained warming maintains pressure but no qualifying signals this cycle. GERP 15-20 percent upward revision remains active baseline.

tipping_point_tracker.key_indicator: El Niño SST anomaly; March 2026 second-warmest on record; IMO 2020 aerosol reduction adds 5-10 percent thermal stress to Great Barrier Reef
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_risk: Biosphere integrity collapse, ocean acidification acceleration, fisheries collapse, coastal protection loss
tipping_point_tracker.cascade_chain: El Niño-driven SST anomalies open bleaching window across Indo-Pacific. Coral bleaching reduces reef structural integrity, eliminating coastal storm protection and collapsing fisheries-dependent food security. Reef collapse accelerates ocean acidification through reduced carbonate buffering capacity.
tipping_point_tracker.source: Copernicus Climate Change Service 2026; Communications Earth and Environment 2026
tipping_point_tracker.asymmetric: C3S Tier 1 confirms El Niño development and second-warmest March SST, opening bleaching window. Tier 2 study quantifies ship sulfur regulation as adding 5-10 percent thermal stress to Great Barrier Reef via aerosol reduction pathway. Stronger precursor signals than baseline, though no in-water bleaching confirmation this week.

climate_security_nexus.conflict_link: Food insecurity drives internal displacement and northward migration pressure, creating political instability in Guatemala, Honduras, El Salvador
climate_security_nexus.cascade_chain: El Niño drought forecast through boreal summer 2026 reduces maize and bean yields in Central America Dry Corridor. Crop failure drives food insecurity, forcing internal displacement and northward migration. Migration pressure creates political instability and governance breakdown in origin and transit states.
climate_security_nexus.asymmetric: Central America Dry Corridor is chronic food insecurity zone; El Niño-driven drought escalates existing cascade. Historical pattern: 2014-2016 El Niño drove migration surge that peaked in 2018-2019.

climate_security_nexus.conflict_link: Transboundary resource tensions in Mekong basin
climate_security_nexus.cascade_chain: El Niño drier conditions forecast for southern and southeastern Asia reduces Mekong River flow. Reduced flow intensifies transboundary water competition among China, Laos, Thailand, Cambodia, Vietnam. Agricultural disruption creates food insecurity and internal displacement pressure.
climate_security_nexus.asymmetric: Mekong basin is chronic transboundary water stress zone; El Niño-driven drought escalates existing tensions. China controls upstream dams, creating asymmetric leverage over downstream states.

climate_security_nexus.conflict_link: Food stress and displacement in Venezuela-Colombia border region
climate_security_nexus.cascade_chain: El Niño drought forecast for northern South America reduces agricultural output in Venezuela and Colombia. Food stress drives displacement across Venezuela-Colombia border, intensifying existing migration crisis and governance breakdown.
climate_security_nexus.asymmetric: Venezuela-Colombia border is chronic displacement zone; El Niño-driven drought escalates existing crisis. Low confidence due to limited Tier 1 data on current food security status.

icj_tracker.significance: ICJ climate advisory opinion will establish legal framework for state obligations under international law to prevent climate harm. Outcome will shape Loss and Damage liability framework and NDC enforcement mechanisms.

loss_damage_tracker.compliance_cycle: No new disbursement data this week
loss_damage_tracker.key_development: No new developments this week. Mechanism operationalised at COP28 but disbursement tracking remains opaque. Committed and disbursed figures not updated this cycle.

reverse_cascade_check.geopolitical_event: No major geopolitical event flagged by ESA or SCEM this week
reverse_cascade_check.note: Reverse cascade check completed. No geopolitical event identified this week that materially accelerates an Earth system boundary transgression. Standard weekly check satisfied.

aerosol_dual_risk_flag.dual_risk_summary: IMO 2020 sulfur regulation reduced aerosol cooling over Great Barrier Reef by 11 W per square meter, increasing thermal stress by 5-10 percent. Dual risk: reduced aerosol cooling increases coral bleaching risk while improving air quality and reducing PM2.5 mortality.

regional_coverage.note: FM-ERM-02 met: 1 South Asia item (Southeast Asia water stress), 2 ocean systems items (coral bleaching precursor, AMOC slowdown). Sub-Saharan Africa and MENA not covered this week due to El Niño geographic focus on Indo-Pacific and tropical Americas.

environmental_governance_composite.note: No new governance data this week. Composite score not updated.

key_judgments.judgment: Three-year average 2023-2025 exceeding 1.5C represents structural acceleration of climate change boundary transgression, not single-year anomaly. Projected sustained breach by February 2034 is nine years ahead of 2015 Paris Agreement baseline, signaling NDC mitigation framework failure.

AMOC slowdown evidence consolidates across four independent mooring arrays with meridionally consistent western boundary decline. Carbon feedback quantification at 47-83 ppm CO2 materially alters long-run physical risk pricing and creates compounding cascade risk across Amazon, Sahel, and South Asian monsoon systems.

C3S El Niño confirmation with second-warmest March SST on record opens compound bleaching and drought cascade risk window across Indo-Pacific and tropical Americas through boreal summer 2026. Coral tipping system proximity assessed as Imminent with High probability preliminary.

IMO 2020 ship sulfur regulation creates dual risk pathway: reduced aerosol cooling increases Great Barrier Reef thermal stress by 5-10 percent while improving air quality. No international liability framework exists for cross-jurisdictional aerosol impacts, creating attribution gap.
